title: Explorer Config
description: Shared domain and event registry for the safety-net-blueprint explorer package. Consumed by context-map and service-blueprints tools.

cross_cutting.description: Cross-cutting concerns shown as a banner on the context-map overview.

actors.description: Human actors used as swimlane participants in flow sequence diagrams.

id.description: Unique identifier. Used as the filename slug and navigation ID. Must be snake_case.

label.description: Display name shown in the diagram.

status.description: Design completion status. Domains with 'not-started' are shown in the overview but are not clickable and do not generate a detail page.

description.description: One-line description of the domain's responsibility.

entities.description: Key entities owned by this domain, shown as a subtitle in the diagram.

events.description: Domain events (asynchronous, pub/sub). Status reflects whether the publisher is emitting the event, not whether individual subscribers have implemented their handlers.

name.description: Event name, conventionally in dot notation (e.g. application.submitted).

publisher.description: Domain ID that emits the event. Must match a domain id in the domains list.

status.description: Implementation status. Applies to all listed subscribers.

subscribers.description: One or more domain IDs that consume the event. Each must match a domain id in the domains list. Empty array is valid for events with no current cross-domain consumers.

apis.description: Direct synchronous API calls (point-to-point, not event-driven). Each entry represents one operation and the domains that invoke it.

call.description: Human-readable name of the operation being invoked.

domain.description: Domain ID that exposes this operation. Must match a domain id in the domains list.

callers.description: One or more domain IDs that invoke this operation. Each must match a domain id in the domains list.

flows.description: Ordered sequences of steps for key business processes. Used to generate sequence diagrams in the context map.

Actor.description: A human participant in a flow diagram, rendered as a named swimlane.

id.description: Unique identifier. Must be snake_case. Used in participants lists and in step from/to fields.

label.description: Display name shown in the swimlane header.

Flow.description: A named sequence of steps representing a key business process.

id.description: Unique identifier. Must be snake_case. Used as the navigation target for ref steps.

label.description: Display name shown in the flow diagram header.

domain.description: Primary domain this flow belongs to. Determines which domain detail page hosts this diagram. Must match a domain id in the domains list.

description.description: One or two sentences describing what this flow covers.

participants.description: Domain and actor IDs in left-to-right swimlane order. Each must match an id in the domains or actors lists.

steps.description: Ordered steps in the sequence diagram.

Step.description: One step in a sequence flow. Must be exactly one of: arrow (from/to), self-loop, event, plain fragment, opt fragment, par fragment, or ref.

RegulatoryItem.description: A regulatory citation or policy source associated with a step. Displayed as a blue badge with a hover tooltip.

citation.description: Regulatory citation or policy source (e.g. '7 CFR § 273.2(g)', 'State policy', 'Workflow convention').

summary.description: One-sentence summary of the requirement as it applies to this step.

detail.description: Optional clarifying detail shown in the tooltip on hover.

OverlayItem.description: A state-overridable policy point on a step. Displayed as an amber ⊕ badge with a hover tooltip.

note.description: Description of what states can configure or replace at this point.

mechanism.description: The overlay artifact a state would edit (e.g. 'rules config', 'state-machine', 'sla-types config').

ArrowStep.description: A directed action or API call from one participant to another, drawn as a labeled arrow.

label.description: Short label shown on the arrow.

from.description: Sending participant ID (domain or actor). Must appear in the flow's participants list.

to.description: Receiving participant ID (domain or actor). Must appear in the flow's participants list.

note.description: Annotation shown beneath the arrow in the diagram.

condition.description: Guard condition shown in [brackets] on the arrow.

regulatory.description: Regulatory citations or policy sources that apply to this step.

gap.description: Marks this step as a known design gap. Displays a red dashed indicator.

gap_description.description: Explanation of the gap shown in the hover tooltip. Only meaningful when gap is true.

overlay.description: State override points at this step. Each entry describes one configurable policy area. Displayed as an amber ⊕ badge.

SelfStep.description: An internal action taken by a single participant, drawn as a self-loop or inline note in its swimlane.

self.description: Participant ID performing the internal action. Must appear in the flow's participants list.

label.description: Short label describing the internal action.

note.description: Annotation shown beneath the label in the diagram.

regulatory.description: Regulatory citations or policy sources that apply to this step.

gap.description: Marks this step as a known design gap.

gap_description.description: Explanation of the gap. Only meaningful when gap is true.

overlay.description: State override points at this step. Each entry describes one configurable policy area. Displayed as an amber ⊕ badge.

EventStep.description: An asynchronous domain event emitted by one participant and received by another, drawn as a dashed arrow.

event.description: Event name, matching an entry in the events registry.

from.description: Publishing participant ID. Must appear in the flow's participants list.

to.description: Subscribing participant ID. Must appear in the flow's participants list.

note.description: Annotation shown beneath the arrow in the diagram.

gap.description: Marks this step as a known design gap.

gap_description.description: Explanation of the gap. Only meaningful when gap is true.

PlainFragment.description: A visual grouping box with no conditional semantics. Groups related steps under an implicit label derived from the fragment identifier.

fragment.description: Unique identifier for this fragment within the flow. Used as the implicit label in the diagram.

OptFragment.description: A conditional block ('opt' in UML sequence diagram notation). The labeled condition is shown in the fragment header; steps inside execute only when the condition is met.

fragment.description: Unique identifier for this fragment within the flow.

label.description: Condition label shown in the fragment header (e.g. 'if SNAP applied').

steps.description: Steps that execute when the condition is met.

ParFragment.description: A parallel block ('par' in UML sequence diagram notation). Operands represent independently triggered branches that occur concurrently.

fragment.description: Unique identifier for this fragment within the flow.

label.description: Label shown in the fragment header.

operands.description: Independent parallel branches. Each operand is a named branch with its own steps.

Operand.description: A named parallel branch inside a par fragment.

label.description: Branch label shown in the par fragment header.

RefStep.description: A clickable navigation box that links to another flow. Shows where the referenced flow fits in context without duplicating its content.

ref.description: The id of the flow this step links to. Must match a flow id in the flows list.

label.description: Display label for the navigation box. Defaults to the referenced flow's label if omitted.
